summary: Matched and linked the Event Yoshi Egg item implementation by replacing placeholder/nonmatching functions in itevyoshiegg with typed code, adding item vars and special-attribute structs, wiring the file as Matching in configure.py, and renaming key symbols from generic it_8029*/it_3F14 names to itEvYoshiEgg-specific names. The implementation adds the item state table, spawn routine, damage-received behavior, countdown animation behavior, and correct xDD4_itemVar union access for evyoshiegg.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itevyoshiegg.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...), linking the file into the matched build.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F6728 in itevyoshiegg.c with six states using itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion0 through UnkMotion5 callbacks.

Implemented itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn(Vec3* pos), replacing the prior placeholder symbol it_8029B0C8. The routine initializes a SpawnItem for It_Kind_EvYoshiEgg, zeroes velocity and damage, sets parent pointers/null flags, calls Item_80268B18, then initializes evyoshiegg item vars and state setup when spawn succeeds.
Changed Logic42_Spawned to use it->xDD4_itemVar.evyoshiegg.xDD4 instead of the previous bombhei union member, removing a comment that indicated the old access was only a stand-in.

Implemented itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion5_Anim as a countdown behavior using evyoshiegg.xDD8, calling gm_801BEB68(1) when the counter equals 40, decrementing the counter, and returning true when the counter expires.

Implemented itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived, replacing the generic it_3F14_Logic42_DmgReceived name. It checks whether the egg has already entered the damage state, compares accumulated damage xC9C against special attribute x0, spawns effect 1231 twice with scale 1.5f, plays sound/effect Item_8026AE84(ip, 244, 0x7F, 0x40), then transitions through a helper that hides the JObj, clears velocity, sets evyoshiegg vars, and changes to item state 5.
Added itEvYoshiEgg_DatAttrs and itEvYoshiEgg_ItemVars to itCommonItems.h, with the item vars containing xDD4 and xDD8 fields used by the matched code.
Added itEvYoshiEgg_ItemVars evyoshiegg to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Updated itevyoshiegg.h prototypes to type itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn(Vec3*) and rename the damage-received prototype to itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived.
Updated item logic table references in it_2725.c and an adjacent documentation/comment block in it_3F14.c to use itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to rename it_8029B0C8 to itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn and it_3F14_Logic42_DmgReceived to itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived, and converted three sdata2 float symbols at 0x804DCC88, 0x804DCC8C, and 0x804DCC90 from global it_804DCCxx names to local @153, @154, and @195 labels.
smart_moves: Introduced item-specific xDD4_itemVar.evyoshiegg storage before replacing placeholder union access, which makes the matched behavior safer and more self-documenting than continuing to reuse bombhei fields.
Renamed only the functions with strong item-specific evidence, while leaving unrelated/shared generic functions such as it_3F14_Logic42_DmgDealt, Reflected, Clanked, and HitShield unchanged.

Kept the damage-received transition setup in a static inline helper, likely to preserve codegen while isolating the repeated state-change sequence: hide model, stop/clear item behavior, zero velocity, set counters, and switch item state.

Updated symbols, headers, item logic tables, and configure.py together, avoiding the common failure mode where a matched C implementation is present but not linked or still referenced by stale generic names.
Localized float constants in symbols.txt as compiler-generated local labels instead of preserving misleading global it_804DCCxx names.
decomp_lessons: When matching an item file, complete the plumbing: mark the object Matching in configure.py, update config symbols, add prototypes, add item-var union members, and update item logic table references.
If existing code accesses an unrelated union member as a placeholder, add a correctly named item-specific var struct and switch all accesses to that member before treating the file as matched.

Item special attributes should be modeled with a typed DatAttrs struct once the function clearly reads xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes; even partial field names such as x0/x4 are preferable to untyped pointer arithmetic when evidence is limited.

Event item damage behavior can depend on an item-specific latch flag plus damage threshold: this PR checks evyoshiegg.xDD4, compares ip->xC9C with a data attribute, then transitions into a hidden/countdown state.
State tables are often required for matching item files; define the ItemStateTable near the item implementation and use NULL where the original callback slot is empty.
Rename symbols to item-specific names when call sites and logic table slots identify ownership, but do not over-rename adjacent shared it_3F14 callbacks without direct evidence.
naming_conventions: Event Yoshi Egg function names use the prefix itEvYoshiEgg_ for logic and spawn functions, for example itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn and itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived.

Existing motion callback names in this slice use the mixed-case prefix itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll, preserving the file's established symbol casing rather than normalizing all names in this PR.
Item-specific data structs follow item prefix plus role: itEvYoshiEgg_DatAttrs and itEvYoshiEgg_ItemVars.
xDD4_itemVar union member name is lowercase item name evyoshiegg.
Unknown fields remain offset-style names such as x0, x4, xDD4, and xDD8 when no semantic name is evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used chained assignment for zeroing spawn velocity components: spawn.vel.x = spawn.vel.y = spawn.vel.z = 0.0f, matching likely compiler output for initialization.
Preserved a static inline helper for the damage-received transition, suggesting code-shape control while keeping the main function readable.
Called efSync_Spawn twice with the same effect id 1231 and same scale variable, indicating the match required two identical effect spawns rather than a loop or helper abstraction.
Kept an apparently unused local f32* unused in itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived, likely because local variable layout or debug/codegen requirements affected matching.
Used Item_80268E5C(gobj, 5, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) to enter the newly defined state table index 5 after setting the countdown fields.
Changed sdata2 float labels from global it_804DCCxx to local @number labels to align the symbol map with compiler-emitted local constants.
review_feedback: No review comments were provided in the PR context. The PR merged quickly and the available dump has no human text excerpt, so reviewer rationale is not evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion* callbacks to more semantic state names.
Search for usages of itEvYoshiEgg_DatAttrs.x0 and x4 to determine proper field names for the damage threshold and pointer field.
Compare other event item damage-received implementations that hide the JObj, clear velocity, set countdown vars, and enter a delayed destruction state.
Check whether it_8029B1D8 and remaining it_3F14 Logic42 shared callbacks were later attributed to Event Yoshi Egg or kept shared across items.
